fullNotes: IPL 2026: The Business of Attention. A critical look at the '300-Hour Trap' for students, the monetization of peak study hours, and the use of publicly-trained athletes for private profit over an 84-match season.

questions.q: IPL 2026 = 336 hours of screen time! For a student, this equals lose of?
questions.explanation: At 6 hours of study/day, 84 matches kill 56 days of productivity. That is the '300-Hour Trap'!

questions.q: 7:30 PM - 11:30 PM 'Prime Time'. Is it for Cricket or for selling your time?
questions.explanation: Matches occupy 'Peak Retention' hours. It prioritizes 'Ad-Revenue' over the nation's human capital.

questions.q: 65 Days & 84 Matches! Is this a Sport or a Revenue Cycle?
questions.a: A business strategy to milk viewership
questions.explanation: More matches mean more ad-slots and subscription renewals. This is 'Attention Economy,' not just sport.

questions.q: Publicly-trained heroes, private-brand profits. Is the 'Publicity Service' fair?
questions.a: No, it's renting public talent for private gain
questions.explanation: Stars built on taxpayer-funded domestic circuits are 'rented' by franchises to build billion-dollar private brands.
